_schema.purpose: Public record of feature requests submitted to external projects. Shown on /feature-requests page so our community can see what we depend on and track resolution.

requests.title: Worktree-spawned agents should inherit parent session's hooks
blocks: Behavioral trace capture for eval runs using worktree isolation
requests.description: When using the Agent tool with isolation: 'worktree', the spawned subagent does not inherit the parent session's PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks. This means no trace events are captured for isolated eval runs — we get the code output but zero process data (tool calls, skill invocations, file mutations). This blocks our core evaluation methodology.
requests.workaround: Clone to a separate repo instead of using worktree isolation. Hooks fire if .claude/settings.json is set up in the clone. More setup overhead but functional.
requests.impact_on_gad: Without this, every eval run produces builds but no behavioral traces. We cannot compare HOW agents work across conditions (GAD vs bare vs emergent) — only WHAT they produce. This undermines the scientific rigor of the evaluation framework.
